channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: OFSS Declares Rs 270 Dividend as EBIT Margins Hit 50.4%
item.description: Oracle Financial Services Software Ltd. (OFSS) has announced a substantial Rs 270 per share dividend, following strong Q4 FY26 results. The company's consolidated net profit rose 38.1% to Rs 842 crore, with revenue up 5.1% sequentially. Notably, EBIT margins expanded sharply to 50.4% from 40.9%, reflecting significant operational gains. OFSS shares climbed 2.47%, defying a 0.81% drop in the Nifty 50.

item.title: HCL Tech Shares Tumble 10% After Revenue Miss, Weak FY27 Forecast
item.description: HCL Technologies reported a 3.3% sequential revenue decline in constant currency for its fourth quarter, missing expectations and showing lower EBIT margins. Despite a 35.4% year-on-year rise in new deal value, the company issued cautious FY27 revenue guidance of 1-4% CC. This led to a nearly 10% stock price drop on April 22, 2026, and prompted many analysts to downgrade the stock, including Jefferies moving to 'Underperform'.

item.title: PNB Housing Finance Beats Estimates with 19% Profit Jump, Analyst Ups Target
item.description: PNB Housing Finance reported strong fourth-quarter fiscal year 2026 results, with profit after tax (PAT) rising 19% year-on-year to INR 6.6 billion, beating analyst expectations. The profit boost came largely from a INR 1.8 billion provision write-back, signaling improved asset quality. Following the results, Motilal Oswal reiterated its 'BUY' rating and increased its price target.

item.title: Persistent Systems Q4 Revenue Miss, AI Costs See Buy Rating Held
item.description: Persistent Systems posted robust full-year fiscal 2026 results, yet the fourth quarter showed a marginal revenue miss and slightly compressed margins due to increased AI investments. Despite these near-term headwinds, analysts largely maintain a positive outlook, reiterating BUY ratings and price targets, citing the company's strategic AI positioning and strong FY26 performance.

item.title: Cyient DLM: Q4 Revenue Falls, But Strong Order Book Promises FY27 Growth
item.description: Cyient DLM reported a year-on-year decline in fourth-quarter fiscal year 2026 revenue and net profit, mainly due to a high prior-year base and geopolitical disruptions. However, the company's order book grew significantly to ₹24.17 billion, signaling strong future revenue visibility. Despite the quarterly revenue challenges, an expanding order pipeline and focus on high-margin segments are expected to drive improved performance and margin stability in fiscal year 2027. This strong backlog appears to be overshadowing short-term earnings pressure, reflected in a positive market reaction.

item.title: India's Urea Costs Surge Amid Conflict, Threatening Food Security
item.description: India is set to purchase 2.5 million tons of urea at significantly higher prices, ranging from $935 to $959 per ton. This represents an almost 90% increase from previous import rates, directly driven by the ongoing Middle East conflict that is disrupting critical supply routes and global fertilizer benchmarks. The timing of this procurement is critical, occurring just before the crucial monsoon sowing season for key crops, potentially impacting agricultural output and food prices.

item.title: India Telecom Adds 9.28M Subscribers as Market Leaders Pull Ahead
item.description: India's telecom sector gained 9.28 million subscribers in March 2026, bringing the total to 1,330.58 million. While broadband also grew, the market shows a widening gap: Reliance Jio and Bharti Airtel are adding users and market share, but Vodafone Idea continues to lose subscribers and faces financial strain. The sector operates amid evolving economic and regulatory landscapes.

item.title: US Extends Russia, Iran Oil Waivers for 30 Days Amid Price Fears
item.description: The U.S. granted a 30-day extension for Russian and Iranian oil sanctions relief, aiming to stabilize global energy prices amid Strait of Hormuz concerns. The move prioritizes immediate price relief for nations like India over weakening sanctioned economies, despite prior signals that waivers would end. This creates policy uncertainty during high geopolitical tensions.

item.title: 360 ONE WAM Revenue Surges 28%, Profit Growth Trails Due to Tax Cloud
item.description: 360 ONE WAM posted a 28% jump in Q4FY26 operating revenue to INR 8.3 billion, fueled by strong transactional and recurring revenue. Net profit grew a slower 17% due to negative other income. The firm also faces a INR 336 crore tax demand it plans to challenge. Motilal Oswal reiterated a 'BUY' rating with a INR 1,300 target.

item.title: Ashok Leyland Bets Big on Hydrogen for 2047 Vision Amid Hurdles
item.description: Ashok Leyland Managing Director and CEO Shenu Agarwal has articulated a long-term vision for the commercial vehicle (CV) industry by 2047, emphasizing a radical shift towards energy independence, software-defined intelligence, and enhanced driver welfare. The strategy pivots from diesel dependency to a multi-fuel approach, with a pronounced focus on green hydrogen, alongside electric, LNG, and bio-CNG. Agarwal forecasts India's potential leadership in hydrogen-powered long-haul trucks, aiming to carry trillions of tonnes of cargo through billions of green kilometers. The vision also embraces intelligent vehicles as "software-defined entities" and elevates driver dignity to professional choice, addressing current shortages.

item.title: ARK Invest Leads $20M Round for B2B Gamification Firm Lucra
item.description: ARK Invest's Venture Fund led its first major investment, heading a $20 million Series B for Lucra. Lucra is a B2B platform that turns corporate loyalty programs into interactive, eSports-style events for customers, with clients like Dave & Busters. This move signals ARK's strategic expansion into early-stage private markets, distinct from its past consumer-gaming focus like Skillz, by emphasizing a business-to-business model. The investment uses ARK's interval fund structure to offer retail investors access to growth opportunities in less liquid assets.

item.title: Air India Faces Soaring Costs as West Asia Conflict Intensifies
item.description: The West Asia conflict is significantly impacting Air India's operations, extending flight times by up to 4 hours and driving up costs, especially fuel (35-40% of expenses). Air India is raising fuel surcharges on international routes. The Indian aviation sector faces a projected ₹17,000-₹18,000 crore loss in FY26 with domestic traffic growth slowing to 0-3%. Competitor IndiGo has lowered its profit outlook and cut operations by ~10%. Jet fuel prices have nearly doubled to ~$195/barrel, straining airline finances.

item.title: Indian MFs Pour Record Cash In as FIIs Flee Amid Market Drop
item.description: Equity mutual funds aggressively deployed capital in March 2026, pushing cash holdings to a 21-month low below ₹2 trillion. This strategic move, representing 13% of reserves, occurred as the Nifty 50 index plunged over 11%. The market's resilience was significantly bolstered by record Domestic Institutional Investor (DII) inflows of $15.4 billion, which more than compensated for $14.2 billion in Foreign Institutional Investor (FII) outflows. This strong domestic liquidity acted as a crucial buffer against global headwinds and foreign selling pressure, underscoring a growing independence of Indian equities from international sentiment.

item.title: GSR's BESO ETF: Active Bitcoin, Ether, Solana With Staking Yields
item.description: Crypto trading firm GSR has launched its first ETF, the GSR Crypto Core3 ETF (BESO), on Nasdaq. This actively managed fund provides diversified exposure to Bitcoin, Ether, and Solana, incorporating staking rewards with a 1% management fee. The move leverages GSR's decade of market expertise, aiming to deliver alpha through dynamic allocation and yield generation in the growing regulated crypto investment space.

item.title: RBI Okays Ex-IRDAI Chief Panda as Bandhan Bank Chairman
item.description: Bandhan Bank has received prior approval from the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) for Debasish Panda to serve as its part-time chairman for a three-year period. Panda, a former chairman of IRDAI and ex-Secretary of the Department of Financial Services, brings extensive experience in banking consolidation and financial inclusion initiatives. This appointment follows the conclusion of Anup Kumar Sinha's term and is subject to further board approval, aligning with the bank's strategic direction. The bank's market capitalization stands at approximately ₹28,374 crore, with a P/E ratio around 28.17x.

item.title: Zerodha Cuts Creator Media Venture Amid India's New Rules
item.description: Zerodha has closed its creator-led media venture, Zero1, due to growing regulatory uncertainty in India's financial content sector. The move signals a shift to a fully owned, in-house content strategy for tighter control over messaging and compliance. This demonstrates Zerodha's dedication to financial education as it navigates a changing regulatory landscape that closely watches financial influencers and digital advice.

item.title: Sigma Sells Stake for $15M to Sharpen Defence Focus
item.description: Sigma Advanced Systems Limited has sold its stake in Extrovis AG for $15 million to focus solely on aerospace and defence manufacturing. This move strengthens its finances, allowing for investments in new capabilities and strategic acquisitions like AS Strategic. While Sigma aims to benefit from India's expanding defence market, some analysts express concerns about its core financial strength.

item.title: US Duty Refunds Create Hurdles for India Exporters Seeking Billions
item.description: The United States has begun refunding reciprocal duties on Indian imports, estimated at $12 billion. However, a critical structural hurdle exists: only U.S.-based importers, who initially paid the tariffs, can file claims. This indirect mechanism leaves Indian exporters without direct legal recourse, forcing them to negotiate with buyers for cost recovery. This introduces significant counterparty risk and potential disputes over the distribution of these funds. The actual benefit for Indian businesses hinges on buyer goodwill and contractual agreements, potentially diluting the intended economic relief.

item.title: RBI Holds Rates Steady Amid Inflation Risks from War, El Niño
item.description: The Reserve Bank of India kept its neutral policy stance and held the repo rate at 5.25%, facing rising inflation risks from the US-Iran conflict and potential El Niño weather. While India's economy is fundamentally strong, revised growth forecasts from international bodies point to a slowdown, highlighting the difficulty in maintaining price stability alongside economic growth.

item.title: LTTS Stock Falls Despite 10% Profit Rise Amid Valuation Worries
item.description: L&T Technology Services (LTTS) reported strong Q4 FY26 results, including a 10% increase in net profit to ₹332 crore and revenue up 2.5% to ₹2,858 crore. The company also announced a ₹40 per share dividend. Despite these positive figures and leadership appointments, LTTS shares fell 2.53%, underperforming the market. Investors appear cautious due to LTTS's high valuation compared to larger IT peers and broader sector shifts driven by Artificial Intelligence.

item.title: RBI Overhauls PPI Rules: Stricter Limits, Wallets Must Connect
item.description: The Reserve Bank of India has rolled out updated regulations for Prepaid Payment Instruments (PPIs), replacing the previous framework. These new directions impose stricter operational, compliance, and customer protection measures on digital wallet and card providers. Key changes include enhanced net worth requirements for non-bank issuers, revised balance and transaction limits for full-KYC wallets, and a mandate for interoperability across platforms. The RBI aims to standardize the digital payments ecosystem while reinforcing risk controls and customer safeguards.

item.title: CCIL Marks 25 Years: Revolutionizing Indian Markets, Embracing Tech Future
item.description: The Clearing Corporation of India Ltd. (CCIL) has marked its 25th anniversary. SBI Chairman C.S. Setty praised CCIL's vital role in transforming India's financial system from manual processes to a sophisticated, tech-driven environment. CCIL has improved efficiency, transparency, and risk management across money, forex, and bond markets. The company is now focused on leveraging technology and data for future growth and market confidence.

item.title: HIVE, Keel Miners Pivot to AI, Face Huge Funding Demands
item.description: HIVE Digital and Keel Infrastructure are aggressively shifting from Bitcoin mining to AI and High-Performance Computing (HPC) infrastructure. HIVE secured $115 million via convertible notes for data center expansion, while Keel is divesting assets for its AI pivot, though the sale fell short of expectations. Both companies saw their stock prices rise approximately 7% following the announcements. This strategic pivot reflects a sector-wide trend driven by declining Bitcoin mining economics and surging AI demand, yet it introduces significant capital demands, operational complexities, and intense market competition.

item.title: India Faces AC Shortage as Heatwave Meets Tougher Efficiency Rules
item.description: India's air conditioner market faces severe shortages as a scorching heatwave clashes with production limits. The latest energy-efficient models, required by new government rules, are especially hard to produce due to their complex design. Retailers are getting only half their orders, and rising costs plus a weaker rupee suggest price hikes are coming. This puts manufacturers in a tough spot, balancing demand with slim supplies during the peak season.

item.title: India Office Market Booms: GCCs, AI Drive Unprecedented Growth
item.description: India's office sector is experiencing structural expansion, not just recovery. Record leasing volumes and net absorption in 2025 and early 2026 underscore robust demand, primarily fueled by Global Capability Centres (GCCs) and evolving AI integration. Vacancy rates are tightening significantly, pushing rental growth across major cities, while the REIT market matures. This performance diverges sharply from the corrective trends seen in developed economies.

item.title: India's Diamond Sector Faces 'Worst Crisis'; Recovery Hopes Rise
item.description: India's diamond industry is grappling with its most severe downturn in 60 years, with exports nearly halving since FY22. Founder-Chairman Govind Dholakia attributes the crisis to market confusion caused by lab-grown diamonds. Despite geopolitical risks, early signs of recovery are emerging, particularly in larger natural diamonds.

item.title: Eveready Launches India's First Alkaline Battery Plant in Jammu
item.description: Eveready Industries has launched India's inaugural alkaline battery manufacturing facility in Jammu, a ₹200 crore investment set to boost its market share from 16% to 25% in three years. The plant significantly reduces import reliance and is projected to improve segment margins by 10%, capitalizing on the over 20% growth in the alkaline battery sector and the increasing demand for high-drain devices. This strategic move positions Eveready to capture a larger slice of the shifting consumer preference away from traditional carbon-zinc batteries.

item.title: Flexi-Cap Funds Split: Managers Choose Stability or Growth Stocks Amidst Volatility
item.description: Flexicap funds are showing a clear split in how they invest across company sizes. Some managers are buying more large-cap stocks for stability. Others are increasing investments in mid- and small-cap (SMID) stocks, looking for growth and attractive valuations. This different strategy highlights varied views on market opportunities and risk appetites. March 2026 data shows 22 of 39 funds increased large-cap holdings, while 17 boosted SMID stocks, pointing to a divided approach to navigating market dynamics.

item.title: Amazon, Aashirwad Cinemas Clash Over Drishyam 3 Rights; OTT IP Value Soars
item.description: The Delhi High Court has issued an interim injunction favouring Amazon Prime Video, restraining Aashirwad Cinemas from transferring digital rights for the upcoming film 'Drishyam 3'. This legal clash highlights the escalating value of exclusive content rights and the fierce competition for intellectual property in India's rapidly maturing Over-The-Top (OTT) market. The dispute centers on Amazon's claim of exclusive renewal rights from a 2020 agreement, a situation reflecting broader industry trends of IP leverage and contractual disputes.

item.title: India-China Flights Resume: Air Connectivity Restored as Trade Improves
item.description: Direct flights between Delhi and Beijing, Kunming-Kolkata, and Kolkata-Shanghai have resumed, restoring vital air links. This move signals renewed economic ties between India and China, following years of tension and pandemic disruptions. It coincides with India easing investment restrictions on China and a rise in bilateral trade, though geopolitical issues persist.

item.title: India's AI Boom: Growth Outpaces Job Quality Amid Deep Skills Gap
item.description: India's economy is powered by digital growth and AI, set to unlock $1.7 trillion by 2035. However, asset prices are rising faster than wages, fueling inequality. Despite a large AI talent pool, a significant skills gap and informal jobs hold back quality employment for educated youth. New opportunities are emerging, but training systems struggle to keep up with rapid tech advances.

item.title: Onboarding Gaps Breed Risk: AI Orchestration Now Crucial
item.description: Enterprise onboarding processes are failing to keep pace with regulatory demands, creating significant compliance vulnerabilities. Fragmented, static workflows lead to oversight gaps, amplifying risk as external and internal actors gain access to systems. While basic automation exists, it lacks the intelligence for contextual validation, making AI-driven, agentic orchestration essential for navigating complex regulations like KYC and AML. Companies must shift from viewing onboarding as administrative to a strategic control point, prioritizing compliance resilience over mere throughput to avoid accumulating systemic vulnerabilities.

item.title: M2P Fintech Hires CFO Vivek Seshadri for IPO Push
item.description: M2P Fintech appointed Vivek Seshadri as Group CFO to strengthen its financial operations and prepare for a potential IPO. Seshadri, formerly of Hitachi Payment Services, brings over 15 years of experience. This move supports M2P's rapid global growth and the trend of fintech infrastructure firms seeking public market access in an investment environment that increasingly values sustainable profitability.

item.title: CBI Raids RCom Office Over LIC Investment Fraud Allegations
item.description: The Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) has seized documents from Reliance Communications' (RCom) Navi Mumbai offices. These records relate to debentures and commercial papers issued to the Life Insurance Corporation of India (LIC) between 2009 and 2016. This action is tied to a new case accusing RCom and Anil Ambani of fraudulently inducing LIC to invest ₹4,500 crore in Non-Convertible Debentures (NCDs), allegedly causing a ₹3,750 crore loss to LIC. The development adds to RCom's severe financial troubles, marked by heavy debt and ongoing insolvency.

item.title: Tata Steel Uses AI to Sharpen Global Edge
item.description: Tata Steel is embedding Google Cloud's AI across its operations, deploying over 300 specialized agents via platforms like 'Zen AI' and TDA. This initiative aims to transform AI from an experiment into an employee-empowering tool for instant insights, improving asset maintenance, cutting HR ticket times by 70%, and reducing customer service response by 50%. The move signals a strategic push for operational agility and scale, positioning the company for enhanced global competitiveness.

item.title: India Approves SAF Blending for Jet Fuel, Delays Domestic Mandates
item.description: India's government has cleared regulations allowing sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) and other synthetic fuels to be blended with jet fuel. Although the aim is to cut emissions and reduce reliance on imported oil, the policy does not set immediate targets for domestic flights. Instead, it follows international timelines, indicating a potentially slower uptake within India compared to global markets and raising concerns about future fuel costs for airlines.

item.title: Tech Mahindra Profit Rises Amid Deal Wins, But Valuation Worries
item.description: Tech Mahindra's March quarter net profit jumped 20.5% sequentially to ₹1,353.8 crore, with revenue up 4.7% to ₹15,076.1 crore, helped by currency gains. Revenue beat estimates, but profit missed forecasts. The company secured strong deal wins, especially for AI projects. Still, its valuation, marked by a high P/E ratio versus rivals, is a key concern.

item.title: India Boosts Energy Security: LPG, PNG Expansion, Gulf Shipping Safe
item.description: India is securing its energy supply by boosting LPG distribution and capacity while expanding Piped Natural Gas (PNG) networks. Strict measures are in place to prevent LPG hoarding. Maritime operations in the Persian Gulf remain safe, with focus on seafarer welfare. The country aims for immediate needs and long-term clean energy goals.

item.title: Trent Reports Strong Q4 Growth, Issues First Bonus Shares Amid Economic Pressures
item.description: Trent Limited reported a strong Q4 FY26 with net profit up 26% to ₹400 crore and revenue up 19% to ₹5,028 crore, driven by store expansion in Tier II/III cities. The company announced its first bonus share issue (1:2 ratio) and a ₹2,500 crore fund-raise. However, growing geopolitical tensions and inflation are creating economic pressures, leading to cautious consumer spending on discretionary items. Key concerns include Trent's high valuation and the time needed for new markets to mature.

item.title: Infosys Partners OpenAI to Accelerate Enterprise AI & Software Modernization
item.description: Infosys is partnering with OpenAI, integrating its advanced AI models like Codex into the Topaz Fabric suite. This collaboration aims to speed up enterprise software development and modernization, helping clients move from AI experiments to practical, large-scale use. The goal is to boost engineering efficiency and shorten time-to-market for services like legacy system updates and DevOps automation, positioning Infosys to compete in the growing enterprise AI market.
